recalls.Description: The DIY Sauna Heater Kit is being recalled due to a significant fire hazard that could lead to serious injury or death. This kit allows consumers to build a heating fixture using four heat-lamp bulbs (sold separately) and a metal enclosure designed for a custom sauna. The kit includes a stainless steel cage, assembly screws and bolts, ceramic bulb sockets, wire connectors, ground pigtails, and a three-prong power cord. When assembled, the cage measures approximately 12 inches high, 12 inches wide, and 8 inches deep, weighing around 16 lbs.
recalls.Title: DIY Cold Plunge Recalls Sauna Heater Kits Due to Fire Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact DIY Cold Plunge via email at orders@diycoldplunge.com or visit www.diycoldplunge.com/pages/recall. You can also find details by clicking on 'Recall Information' at the bottom of the homepage.

Products.Name: DIY Sauna Heater Kit
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 675 (In addition, about 14 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled DIY Sauna Heater Kit (shown assembled in sauna)
Recalled DIY Sauna Heater Kit (product photo from diycoldplunge.com)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 12 reports of units overheating. No fires or injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Plunge Buddy LLC, doing business as DIY Cold Plunge, of North Mankato, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.diycoldplunge.com from March 2025 to February 2026, priced between $400 and $500.

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to overheating electrical conductors

Remedies.Name: Stop using the Sauna Heater Kit immediately and register at www.diycoldplunge.com/pages/recall for a full refund. Proof of destruction or a request for a prepaid shipping label for return is required.

recalls.metaDescription: DIY Cold Plunge recalls Sauna Heater Kits due to fire hazards. Stop use immediately and register for a refund.

recalls.Description: A recall has been issued for Aroeve air purifiers, specifically model MK04, available in black and white. These units, manufactured before July 2025, can be identified by a serial number beginning with 'BN' located on the bottom label of the device. Consumers are advised to check their products for this information.
recalls.Title: Airova Recalls Aroeve Air Purifiers Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out via email at Aroeve-airpure-recall@outlook.com or visit https://aroeve.com/pages/product-recall-information. You can also go to https://aroeve.com/ and select 'Product Recalls' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Aroeve Air Purifier MK04
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 191,390

Images.Caption: Recalled Aroeve MK04 Air Purifier in white
Recalled Aroeve MK04 Air Purifier in black
Product label with model, date code, and serial number

Injuries.Name: Airova received 37 reports of the air purifiers overheating, including one report of fire. No injuries or property damage have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, Shopify.com, TEMU.com, and TikTok.com from September 2024 to June 2025, priced between $80 and $134.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating

Remedies.Name: Free replacement air purifier

recalls.metaDescription: Airova recalls Aroeve air purifiers due to fire hazards. Check your model for safety and contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Electric motors designed for gable and roof-mounted attic fans are being recalled due to a potential fire hazard. These motors were sold between August 2003 and December 2013 and can be identified by their unique vent pattern on the motor end bells. The affected model numbers include DOW-136-0-34-XIN, DOW-136-0-26-XIN, DOW-136-0-40-XIN, DOW-136-0-40-XIN-3/4", and D-RE-PSC-127/20-4P1SP. Only motors featuring this specific vent pattern are included in the recall.
recalls.Title: Recall of Electric Motors for Gable and Roof-Mounted Attic Fans Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Air Vent at 877-247-4878, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional information is accessible online at https://powermaxmotorrecall.expertinquiry.com or https://gibraltarbuildingproducts.com/ventilation-category/ under "Important Safety Information."

Products.Name: Gable and Roof-Mounted Attic Fan Electric Motors
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2.9 million

Images.Caption: Sample Roof-Mounted Attic Fan with Recalled Electric Motor
Sample Gable-Mounted Attic Fan with Recalled Electric Motor

Recalled Electric Motor Vent Patterns and Unaffected Motor Vent Patterns (Recalled electric motor vents (depicted on the left) align on the outer ring of the motor end bell, as highlighted in yellow. Recalled electric motor vents use an alternating pattern of two long vents then two short vents. Unaffected motor vents (depicted on the right) align on the inner ring of the motor end bell, as highlighted in yellow. Unaffected motor vents use an alternating vent pattern of one long, then one short vent)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 159 reports of incidents related to the recalled attic fan motors involving burning or fire. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at various retailers and wholesalers nationwide, including ABC Supply, Lowe's, and Beacon, from August 2003 to December 2013, priced between $74 and $92.

Distributors.Name: Air Vent Inc., of Dallas, Texas

Hazards.Name: Risk of short circuit and fire due to motor's safety cutoff failure

Remedies.Name: Stop using the motors and contact Air Vent for a refund by submitting an online registration and verification form with photos.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert for electric motors used in attic fans due to fire hazards. Contact Air Vent for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description:
IBC Technologies has announced a recall of their CX Series Combi Boilers, which are designed for residential use, providing both central heating and hot water. These compact gas boilers are identifiable by their rectangular shape and a control panel located on the front, featuring a red IBC label. The recall specifically affects models CX-199 and CX-150 with designated serial numbers manufactured between April 2023 and April 2025. Consumers should check the model and serial numbers located on the unit's rating plate inside the upper left corner to determine if their boiler is included in the recall.

The affected CX Combi Boilers could allow hot water to exceed the temperature set on the control panel, creating a risk of burns. IBC Technologies urges customers to contact them for a free in-home repair, which may include the installation of a mixing valve to resolve the issue. Meanwhile, users can safely continue using their boilers for central heating purposes.
recalls.Title: IBC Technologies Recalls CX Series Combi Boilers Due to Potential Burn Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach IBC Technologies at 844-432-8422 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email sales@ibcboiler.com, or visit www.ibcboiler.com/recalls for more information.

Products.Name: CX Series Combi Boiler
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,100 (In addition, about 880 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled IBC CX 199 Combi Boiler
Location of Model Number and Serial Number on the Rating Plate Inside the Recalled CX Combi Boiler

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of the hot water temperature fluctuating while in use. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through home improvement distributors nationwide from May 2023 to June 2025, priced between $4,700 and $5,100.

Importers.Name: IBC Technologies USA Inc., of Lawnside, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Potential burn hazard from excessively hot water

Remedies.Name: Free in-home repair and mixing valve installation if necessary

recalls.metaDescription: IBC Technologies recalls CX Series Combi Boilers due to burn hazards. Contact for free repairs and ensure your safety today.

recalls.Description: Trane and American Standard are recalling several models of Gas/Electric Packaged Units due to a potential gas leak and fire hazard. The affected models include specific serial number ranges, which are located on the back of the units. The brands are clearly marked on the front of each unit for easy identification.
recalls.Title: Trane and American Standard Recall Gas/Electric Packaged Units Due to Gas Leak and Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Trane or American Standard at 800-889-0129, available Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET. More information can also be found online at https://www.trane.com/residential/gas-electric-packaged-unit-recall/ or www.americanstandardair.com/gas-electric-packaged-unit-recall/.

Products.Name: Trane Gas/Electric Packaged Unit
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 4,790

Images.Caption: Recalled American Standard Gas/Electric Packaged Unit
Recalled Trane Two-Stage Gas/Electric Packaged Unit
Recalled Two-Stage Gas/Electric Packaged Unit
Model and serial number location

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of gas leaking from the recalled two-stage gas furnace/air conditioners. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Trane U.S. Inc., of Davidson, North Carolina

Retailers.Name: HVAC distributors and dealers across the U.S. from January 2024 to March 2025, priced between $8,250 and $12,900.

Hazards.Name: Unexpected gas valve opening leading to gas leaks and fire hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the heating mode and contact your dealer for a free inspection and ignition board replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Trane and American Standard recall Gas/Electric Packaged Units due to gas leak and fire hazards. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: NTI is recalling its TRX Series II residential wall-mounted gas boilers, specifically models TRX150, TRX199, and TRX199C. These boilers, which are gray and approximately 20 inches wide and 34 inches high, may have faulty burner door studs that prevent the door from sealing correctly. This defect can lead to the escape of flames and harmful gases, creating serious fire and carbon monoxide poisoning hazards that could potentially result in severe injury or death. The model and serial numbers can be found on the rating plate located on the left side of the boiler.
recalls.Title: Recall of NTI Boilers Due to Fire and Carbon Monoxide Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers should contact NTI Boilers at 800-688-2575, available from 7 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email recall@ntiboilers.com or visit http://www.ntiboilers.com/trx-safety-recall. More details can also be accessed by clicking on 'Urgent Recall' on the NTI website.

Products.Name: NTI TRX150 Gas Boiler
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 323 (In addition, 459 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled TRX Series II boiler

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Independent heating and plumbing contractors and wholesale distributors nationwide

Hazards.Name: Fire and Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Risks

Remedies.Name: Corrective Action for Boiler Door Sealing Issue

RemedyOptions.Option: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled boilers and contact NTI Boilers to arrange to have a certified technician install the repair at no cost to the consumer. Consumers who must continue using the boiler while awaiting repair should have working smoke and carbon monoxide alarms in their homes.

recalls.metaDescription: NTI recalls TRX Series II gas boilers due to fire and CO hazards. Contact NTI for safety information.

recalls.Description: HTP is recalling its Elite Ultra residential wall-mounted gas boilers, specifically model numbers ELU-150WBN and ELU-199WBN, due to serious safety hazards. These black boilers, measuring approximately 20 inches wide and 34 inches high, may have faulty burner door studs that prevent the door from sealing properly. This defect can lead to the escape of flames and harmful gases, posing a risk of fire and carbon monoxide poisoning, which could result in severe injury or death.
recalls.Title: HTP Elite Ultra Gas Boilers Recalled for Fire and Carbon Monoxide Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact HTP at 800-323-9651 from 7 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@htproducts.com, or visit https://htproducts.com/safety-notice.html. More information is also available at www.htproducts.com under the 'Urgent Recall' section.

Products.Name: HTP Elite Ultra Gas Boiler ELU-150WBN
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 96 (In addition, 9 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled HTP-brand ELU Elite Ultra boiler

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Sold through independent heating and plumbing contractors and wholesale distributors nationwide from October 2024 to February 2025, priced between $3,400 and $4,100.

Importers.Name: Ariston USA LLC, of Providence, Rhode Island

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and carbon monoxide poisoning due to improper sealing of the boiler door.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled boilers and contact HTP for a free repair installation by a certified technician. If continued use is necessary, ensure functional smoke and carbon monoxide alarms are present.

recalls.metaDescription: HTP recalls Elite Ultra gas boilers due to fire and carbon monoxide risks. Immediate action required for consumer safety.

recalls.Description: Midea is recalling approximately 1.7 million U and U+ Window Air Conditioners due to potential mold exposure. These units, sold under various brand names including Midea, Comfort Aire, Danby, Frigidaire, Insignia, Keystone, LBG Products, Mr. Cool, Perfect Aire, and Sea Breeze, are designed for window installation and come in cooling capacities of 8,000, 10,000, and 12,000 BTU. The air conditioners are white, measuring about 22 inches wide and 14 inches high, with the brand name displayed on the unit. Model numbers are located on a label on the front right side of the unit.
recalls.Title: Midea Recalls 1.7 Million U and U+ Window Air Conditioners Due to Mold Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Midea at 888-345-0256 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email midea4028@midea.com, or visit www.MideaUrecall.expertinquiry.com or www.midea.com/us and click on 'RECALL INFORMATION' for further details.

Products.Name: U and U+ Window Air Conditioners
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1.7 million (In addition, about 45,900 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled U Window Air Conditioner
Recalled U+ Window Air Conditioner

Injuries.Name: The firm is aware of at least 152 reports of mold in the air conditioners, including 17 reports of consumers experiencing symptoms such as respiratory infections, allergic reactions, coughing, sneezing and/or sore throats from mold exposure.

Manufacturers.Name: GD Midea Air-Conditioning Equipment Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available at Costco, Menards, Home Depot, Best Buy, and other retailers nationwide, as well as online at Midea.com, Amazon.com, Costco.com, Menards.com, HomeDepot.com, Lowes.com, Walmart.com, BJs.com, BestBuy.com, and other websites from March 2020 to May 2025, priced between $280 and $500.

Importers.Name: Midea America Corp., of Parsippany, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Risk of mold growth due to inadequate drainage of pooled water, potentially leading to respiratory issues or infections.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should contact Midea for a free repair or a full or prorated refund based on the purchase date. To receive a refund, consumers may need to return the unit using a provided shipping label or send a photo of the cut power cord. For those using the air conditioners while awaiting repairs, instructions for inspection are available at www.MideaUrecall.expertinquiry.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Midea recalls 1.7 million U and U+ Window Air Conditioners due to mold risk. Contact Midea for repairs or refunds.

recalls.Description: Rinnai America is recalling its i-Series Plus residential wall-mounted gas boilers, specifically models IP175S, IP199S, IP175199C, and IP199199C. These boilers are identifiable by a white label on the side that states 'MADE IN JAPAN' along with model numbers and a reference code. They feature a rectangular metal design with the Rinnai name printed in black at the top and a temperature display/keypad located near the bottom front.
recalls.Title: Rinnai America Issues Recall for i-Series Plus Boilers Due to Carbon Monoxide Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Rinnai America Corporation at 800-621-9419 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email at recall@rinnai.us, or visit https://www.rinnai.us/support/recalls for further details.

Products.Name: Rinnai i-Series Plus Residential Gas Boilers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,030 (In addition, 249 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled i-Series Plus Boiler
Recalled i-Series Plus Boiler Label

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available through independent heating and plumbing contractors, wholesale distributors nationwide, and online retailers including Amazon.com, Homedepot.com, and Lowes.com from September 2024 to February 2025, priced between $3,900 and $5,000.

Importers.Name: Rinnai America Corporation, of Peachtree City, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Risk of carbon monoxide poisoning due to improper sealing of the boiler door.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the affected boilers and reach out to Rinnai for a free repair installation by a certified technician. If continued use is necessary, ensure functioning carbon monoxide alarms are installed on each level of the home and outside sleeping areas.

recalls.metaDescription: Rinnai recalls i-Series Plus boilers due to carbon monoxide risks. Contact Rinnai for repairs and safety information.

recalls.Description: All models of Delta-branded Gas Ball Valves are being recalled due to potential fire and burn hazards. These chrome, manually operated gas valves, manufactured by Masco Canada Limited and Delta Faucet Company, are typically used in laboratories within schools, research facilities, and hospitals. Each valve features the name "Delta" on one side and the word "GAS" in white on the blue top. The recalled models can also be identified by the markings "1/2 PSI MAX" and "GAS/GAZ" on the bottom of the unit.
recalls.Title: Recall of Delta Gas Ball Valves Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Masco Canada at 888-789-6779 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@mascocanada.com, or visit https://www.mascocanada.com/recall-notices or www.mascocanada.com and navigate to the "Recalls" section at the bottom of the homepage.

Products.Name: Delta Gas Ball Valves
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3,700 (In addition, about 18,500 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled gas ball valve
Recalled gas ball valve
Recalled gas ball valve
Recalled gas ball valve
Recalled gas ball valve
Recalled gas ball valve with “1/2 PSI MAX” and “GAS/GAZ” identifiers

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively at plumbing supply distributors across the nation from January 2006 to January 2025, priced between $30 and $160.

Importers.Name: Delta Faucet Company, of Indianapolis, Indiana

Hazards.Name: Potential gas leaks from the valve when open, leading to fire and burn risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled valves immediately and contact Masco Canada to verify if their product is affected and to receive refund instructions. Masco Canada will also cover the labor costs for removal and disposal of the valves. The company is reaching out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Delta Gas Ball Valves recalled due to fire and burn hazards. Check if your valve is affected and learn how to get a refund.

recalls.Description: Meijer has announced a recall of the Konwin desktop heaters, identified by model number BHN2133. These heaters feature a white plastic body with a black fan and grill, measuring approximately 12 inches in height and 10 inches in width. The brand name 'Konwin' along with the model number can be found on a label located on the bottom of the unit.
recalls.Title: Meijer Issues Recall for Konwin Desktop Heaters Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to contact Meijer at 800-927-8699, use the contact form available at Web Form, or visit https://help.meijer.com/csp?id=m_kb_recall or https://www.meijer.com/ and navigate to 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: Konwin Desktop Heater
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 6,050

Images.Caption: Recalled Konwin Desktop Heater
Recalled Konwin Desktop Heater label

Injuries.Name: The firm received eight reports of fans burning and melting. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Meijer locations across Michigan, Ohio, Indiana, Illinois, Wisconsin, and Kentucky from September 2024 to January 2025, priced between $25 and $40.

Importers.Name: Meijer Distribution Inc., of Grand Rapids, Michigan

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and burns due to potential overheating from fan failure.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the heaters immediately and return them to any Meijer store for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Meijer recalls Konwin desktop heaters over fire hazards. Learn how to return for a refund and stay safe.

recalls.Description: Triangle Tube has issued a recall for its Prestige Solo, Prestige Excellence, and Aerco Esteem gas boilers, which are wall-mounted condensing models. These units, identifiable by the "Prestige" or "Aerco" labels on their front panels, pose a serious carbon monoxide (CO) poisoning hazard due to potential delayed ignitions or component failures. The recall affects specific models manufactured between December 2011 and April 2019, and consumers are urged to check their serial numbers for eligibility.
recalls.Title: Triangle Tube Recalls Prestige and Aerco Esteem Gas Boilers Due to Carbon Monoxide Risks; Two Fatalities Reported
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Triangle Tube at 877-574-5036 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at productrecall@triangletube.com. More information is available online at https://triangletube.com/products/product-recall/ or by visiting www.triangletube.com and selecting "PRODUCT RECALL."

Products.Name: Prestige Solo Gas Boiler
Products.NumberOfUnits: Approximately 60,000 (In addition, about 7,500 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Prestige Excellence Boiler
Recalled Prestige Solo Boiler
Recalled Prestige Solo Trimax Boiler
Recalled Aerco Esteem Boiler
Recalled Aerco Esteem Watts Brand Boiler

Injuries.Name: Triangle Tube has received two reports of death due to CO poisoning associated with the recalled boilers. In 2015, a 47-year-old woman died from CO poisoning associated with a Prestige Solo 175 boiler, and in 2016, an 85-year-old man died from CO poisoning associated with a Prestige SOLO TriMax 175 boiler, following a repair. Triangle Tube has also received 25 reports of CO leaks from the recalled boilers.

Retailers.Name: These boilers were produced by Burnsen SA in Belgium and sold through wholesale distributors and independent contractors across the country, with prices ranging from $3,400 to $9,700.

Importers.Name: Triangle Tube/Phase III Co. Inc., of West Deptford, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Risk of carbon monoxide (CO) release due to ignition delays or damaged components, leading to potential serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should contact Triangle Tube for a free boiler inspection and, if necessary, a complimentary installation of a new ignition system. While awaiting repairs, it is crucial to have functioning carbon monoxide alarms on every level of the home and near sleeping areas. Those without an alarm can request one from Triangle Tube at no cost.

recalls.metaDescription: Triangle Tube recalls Prestige and Aerco Esteem gas boilers due to CO poisoning hazards. Check your model and serial number for safety.

recalls.Description: Vornado is recalling VH2 Whole Room Heaters identified by the date codes "JUL24" or "AUG24" found on the silver rating label beneath the unit. These off-white heaters feature thermostatic controls, automatic or continuous fan settings, and two heat levels. The front of the heater displays the brand name 'Vornado' with a distinctive red 'V'. Only units marked with the specified date codes are affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: Vornado Recalls VH2 Whole Room Heaters Due to Fire and Electric Shock Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled heaters and reach out to Vornado at 844-205-7978 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email vh2recall@vornado.com, or visit www.vornado.com/recalls/vh2 to verify if their heater is included in the recall and to obtain instructions for a free replacement.

Manufacturers.Name: Vornado Air LLC, of Andover, Kansas

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from August 2024 through October 2024 for approximately $90.

Hazards.Name: Risk of electric shock and fire due to potential power cord detachment

Remedies.Name: Free replacement heater upon confirmation of recall eligibility

recalls.metaDescription: Vornado recalls VH2 Whole Room Heaters over fire and electric shock hazards. Check if your heater is affected and get a free replacement.

recalls.Description: GoveeLife and Govee have issued a recall for their smart electric space heaters, specifically models H7130 (including H7130101), H7131, H7132, H7133, H7134, and H7135. Each heater is marked with 'GoveeLife' or 'Govee' on the front or side and comes in black or white, with heights ranging from 9 to 26 inches. The model number can be found on the manufacturer's label located on the bottom of the unit.
recalls.Title: Recall of GoveeLife and Govee Smart Electric Space Heaters Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled heaters immediately and contact GoveeLife or Govee at 833-772-5360, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. More information can be found online at https://us.govee.com or www.goveelife.com by clicking on 'Recall Information,' or visit https://recall.goveelife.com/heater-recall to participate in the recall.

recalls.Description: Caframo Limited has issued a recall for the model 9421 Cabinet Heaters, designed to maintain warmth in cabinets and parking ticket dispensers. These heaters feature a black steel enclosure and measure approximately 5-3/4 inches wide, 9 inches long, and 3-1/3 inches high. The top of the heater includes a serial plate, an on/off switch, and a label indicating 'Model 9421' near the power cord.
recalls.Title: Caframo 9421 Cabinet Heaters Recalled Due to Electrocution Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Caframo Limited at 800-567-3556 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email contactus@caframo.com. More details are available at https://www.caframo.com/recall/ or by visiting www.caframo.com and selecting 'RECALL' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Caframo 9421 Cabinet Heater
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,140 (In addition, 200 units were sold in Canada)

Top view showing location of Product Model & Serial number
Detailed view showing serial number and model number locations

Retailers.Name: Graybar Electric Co. in St. Louis, MO; Keystone Automotive Operations Inc. in Exeter, PA; Hamilton Manufacturing Corp. in Holland, OH; Controlled Access of the Midwest in Pleasant Hill, IA; available online at comfortsolutions.caframobrands.com and Amazon.com from October 2023 through May 2024 for approximately $150.

Hazards.Name: Improperly finished wire crimp can detach, leading to electrocution risk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the heater, unplug it, and contact Caframo for free repair or replacement instructions. A pre-paid shipping label will be provided for returns. If unrepairable, a replacement will be sent. Caframo is reaching out to known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Caframo recalls model 9421 Cabinet Heaters due to electrocution hazards. Immediate action recommended for consumers.

recalls.Description: Glen Dimplex Americas has announced a recall of its Cadet-branded Apex72 high wall electric heaters, which range from 1200 to 1600 Watts. These fan-forced heaters are designed to be installed at least six feet above the ground and feature a white dust cover. The model number, brand name, and manufacturing date in DD-MM-YY format can be found on the rating label located at the bottom of the air outlet grill. Affected models include HW128, HW132, HW151, and HW162, among others, with specific manufacturing dates outlined in the recall notice.
recalls.Title: Glen Dimplex Americas Issues Recall for Cadet Apex72 Electric Heaters Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Glen Dimplex Americas at 866-673-9880 from 8 a.m. to 6:30 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email apex@glendimplexamericas.zendesk.com, or visit https://www.glendimplexamericas.com/en-us/product-safety-notices/2024-APEX72-Important-Recall-Information for further details.

Products.Name: Cadet Apex72 Electric Heaters
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,900

Images.Caption: Recalled Cadet Apex Electric Heater
Model and manufacturing date location
Cadet, the model and the manufacturing date, in DD-MM-YY format, appear on the rating label visible through the bottom of the air outlet grill

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a heater fan malfunctioning and causing the unit to ignite. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Glen Dimplex Americas Ltd., of Vancouver, Washington

Retailers.Name: Available at Platt, Crescent Electric, CED, and various electrical distributors nationwide from February 2014 through February 2024 for approximately $190.

Hazards.Name: Potential overheating and internal ignition due to fan malfunction

Remedies.Name: Immediate cessation of use, with free replacement and installation of a new heater provided by Glen Dimplex

recalls.metaDescription: Glen Dimplex recalls Cadet Apex72 Electric Heaters due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a free replacement and installation.

recalls.Description: Atomi has issued a recall for several models of Smart Heaters, including tower models AT1323, AT1481, AT1520, and AT1632; tabletop models AT1482 and AT1521; and the wall heater model AT1635. These portable electric heaters are available in black, silver, or white, depending on the specific model. The tower models stand approximately 26-28 inches tall and 8-11 inches wide, while the tabletop models are about 18 inches tall and 8 inches wide. The wall heater measures around 17 inches tall and 23 inches wide. You can find the model number on a product identification sticker located on the bottom of each unit.
recalls.Title: Atomi Smart Heaters Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Atomi at 888-770-7140 from 7:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.atomiheaterrecall.com or www.atomismart.com and click on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: Atomi Smart Tower Heater AT1323
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 99,400 (In addition, about 10,700 were sold in Canada)

Recalled Atomi Smart Tower Heater models AT1481 and AT1520
Recalled Atomi Smart Tabletop Heater models AT1482 and AT152
Recalled Atomi Smart Tower Heater model AT1632
Recalled Atomi Smart Wall Heater model AT1635

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the heater turning on without user input. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Amazon

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to heaters activating without user input

Remedies.Name: Stop using the heaters immediately, unplug them, and follow online instructions at www.atomiheaterrecall.com to obtain a full refund. Consumers must cut the power cord of the unplugged unit and upload photos of the cut cord and the manufacturer's label during the registration process.

recalls.metaDescription: Atomi recalls Smart Heaters due to potential fire and burn hazards. Immediate action required for refunds.

recalls.Description: Daikin Comfort Technologies is recalling specific models of Daikin FIT, Amana S-Series, and Goodman SD heat pumps due to a potential risk of excessive heat exposure. These beige units have the model name displayed on the exterior, with the model number located on the serial plate on the side. The affected models include various configurations within the Daikin FIT, Amana S-Series, and Goodman SD lines.
recalls.Title: Daikin Comfort Technologies Recalls Daikin FIT, Amana S-Series, and Goodman SD Heat Pumps Due to Excessive Heat Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact DCT toll-free at 855-545-0235 between 8 a.m. and 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit http://www.goodmanmfg.com/fit-recall or https://daikincomfort.com/ and select 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: Daikin FIT Heat Pump

Hazards.Name: Risk of excessive heat exposure due to power interruption

Remedies.Name: Remote software update for WiFi-connected systems; registration for non-WiFi systems

recalls.metaDescription: Daikin recalls heat pumps due to excessive heat risk. Check model numbers and contact for software updates.

recalls.Description: Daikin Comfort Technologies is recalling packaged air conditioning and heat pump units under the Amana, Daikin, and Goodman brands. These units, available in gray or green, can be identified by their model numbers located on the electrical connection side of the unit. To determine if your unit is affected, please visit www.goodmanmfg.com/package-recall for more details.
recalls.Title: Daikin Comfort Technologies Recalls Air Conditioning and Heat Pump Units Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact DCT toll-free at 833-324-5460 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.goodmanmfg.com/package-recall. You can also find more information by clicking on 'PRODUCT RECALL' on their website.

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: Daikin Comfort Technologies Manufacturing L.P. (f/k/a Goodman Manufacturing Company L.P.), of Houston, Texas

Retailers.Name: Independent heating and cooling equipment dealers across the nation from January 2024 to March 2024, priced between $4,500 and $7,500.

Hazards.Name: Incorrect electrical ratings on the unit's serial plate may lead to improper wiring, creating a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should refrain from opening the outside electrical panel and contact Daikin Comfort Technologies Manufacturing L.P. (DCT) for a complimentary inspection and repair. DCT is reaching out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Daikin recalls Amana, Daikin, and Goodman air conditioning units due to fire hazards. Check if your unit is affected and learn how to get a free repair.

recalls.Description: Crown Boiler Company is recalling its Aruba IV (AWR Series) gas-fired hot water boilers, specifically those designed for use at elevations exceeding 2,000 feet. These units, which are red and gray and stand 39.5 inches tall, may pose a serious carbon monoxide poisoning hazard if the blocked vent switch fails to operate correctly. The model name Aruba IV is not explicitly labeled on the units, but the model numbers can be found on the rating plate on the right side of the boiler. The affected models include a range of AWR numbers, regardless of their manufacturing date.
recalls.Title: Crown Boiler Recalls Aruba IV Gas-Fired Hot Water Boilers Due to Carbon Monoxide Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Crown Boiler Company at 888-583-5624, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, inquiries can be sent via email to AKP@velocityboilerworks.com or through their website at www.velocityboilerworks.com/news/2024/01/product-modification-required-crown-aruba-iv-models-above-2000-ft-recall/ where further details can be found.

Products.Name: Crown Aruba IV (AWR Series) Gas-Fired Hot Water Boilers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,100

Label location on recalled Crown Boiler gas-fired AWR Series

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: Crown Boiler Company LLC, of Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

Retailers.Name: Available through heating and plumbing contractors and wholesale distributors nationwide from July 2012 to December 2023, priced between $2,000 and $4,000.

Hazards.Name: Risk of carbon monoxide poisoning due to blocked vent switch failure

Remedies.Name: Free repair including replacement of main burner gas orifice and blocked vent switch

recalls.metaDescription: Crown Boiler recalls Aruba IV gas-fired hot water boilers due to carbon monoxide hazards. Contact for free repairs and safety information.

recalls.Description:
U.S. Boiler Company is recalling several models of gas-fired residential boilers due to a potential carbon monoxide poisoning hazard. This recall affects boilers from brands including U.S. Boiler, New Yorker Boiler, Advantage, FORCE, and Archer, specifically those installed at elevations exceeding 5,400 feet. The affected models include the ES2, Series 3, X-2, Lancer, and FORCE, each identifiable by their unique colors and logos. Model and serial numbers can be found on the boiler rating plates, which are located on the top panel or inside the vestibule of the units.

The blocked vent switch may fail to shut down the burners if the vent system becomes obstructed, leading to dangerous levels of carbon monoxide in the home. Consumers are urged to act quickly to ensure their safety and that of their families.
recalls.Title: U.S. Boiler Company Recalls Gas-Fired Hot Water Boilers Due to Carbon Monoxide Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact U.S. Boiler at 888-432-8887 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can reach out via email at info@usboiler.net or visit https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/catsy.782/high+altitude+homeowner+recall+letter3.pdf, usboiler.net, archerboiler.com, or newyorkerboiler.com, and click on "Safety Recall Information: High Altitude Gas Boilers" for more details.

recalls.Description:
The recall affects specific models of Camplux portable tankless water heaters, including AY132, BD158, BW211, BW264, and BW422, manufactured on or before various dates in late 2021. These heaters are designed for outdoor use and are available in white, black, and silver/gray colors. Each unit features the 'Camplux' logo on the front and has a product label on the side detailing model and serial numbers.
Consumers should photograph the label on their device and send it to Camplux to verify if their water heater is part of the recall. This will enable them to receive a replacement gas regulator to enhance safety.
recalls.Title: Camplux Portable Tankless Water Heaters Recalled Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact The Home Appliances (Camplux) at 844-538-7475 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email support@camplux.com. More information is available online at www.thehomeappliances.com under the 'Recalls' section or directly at www.thehomeappliances.com/waterheaterrecall.

Products.Name: Camplux AY132 Portable Tankless Water Heater

Injuries.Name: Camplux has received 19 reports of fire incidents involving the recalled water heaters. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com

Importers.Name: The Home Appliances Inc., d/b/a Camplux, of North Charleston, South Carolina

Hazards.Name: Gas leakage from the regulator connection posing a fire hazard

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled heaters and request a free repair kit, which includes a replacement gas regulator with easy connect features and installation instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: Important recall for Camplux portable tankless water heaters due to fire hazards. Check if your model is affected and learn how to get a free repair.

recalls.Description: Daikin Comfort Technologies Manufacturing (formerly Goodman Manufacturing Company L.P.) has expanded its recall to include additional evaporator coil drain pans manufactured between January 2019 and February 2020. These units were incorrectly labeled with a 2018 serial number. The recall now affects approximately 3,600 additional units, which can be identified by specific coil serial numbers and configurations as detailed below. Consumers should check their units against this expanded list to ensure safety.
recalls.Title: Daikin Comfort Technologies Expands Recall of Evaporator Coil Drain Pans Due to Fire Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach DCT toll-free at 888-520-0579 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.recallrtr.com/drainpan or www.goodmanmfg.com and click on "PRODUCT RECALL" for further details.

Products.Name: Evaporator coil drain pans
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3,600 additional units (In addition, about 12 were sold in Canada) (298,300 evaporator coil drain pans, and 44,000 in Canada, were previously recalled in November 2021 and in July 2022)

Images.Caption: Furnace configurations for recalled evaporator coil drain pans showing an up-flow coil configuration
Recalled evaporator coil drain pans installed with condensing (white pipe) or non-condensing (metal pipe) gas furnaces that are not in an “up-flow” coil configuration
Location of label showing serial number on cased evaporator coils
Location of label showing serial number on uncased evaporator coils
Location of Energy Guide label showing furnace brand and model number
Location of model number on outdoor condenser

Injuries.Name: DCT has received a total of 31 reports of fire involving the drain pans, including five reports since the recall was reannounced in July 2022. The incidents resulted in one reported smoke inhalation and several reports of significant fire and smoke damage to residences.

Manufacturers.Name: Daikin Comfort Technologies Manufacturing L.P. (f/k/a Goodman Manufacturing Company L.P.) of Houston, Texas

Retailers.Name: Independent heating and cooling equipment dealers across the country from January 2019 to November 2021, with prices ranging from $200 to $1,200 per unit. Consumers may have bought these as part of a bundled product including the coil, furnace, installation, and other components, or as a standalone replacement.

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to overheating, melting, and deformation of the molded plastic drain pan.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should contact Daikin Comfort Technologies to verify if their unit is affected and to arrange for a free repair by a qualified technician. DCT will also reach out to registered consumers whose gas furnace systems are included in the recall.

recalls.metaDescription: Daikin Comfort Technologies recalls evaporator coil drain pans due to fire risks. Check if your unit is affected and learn how to get a free repair.
